title: Kiel plaĉas al vi: komedio en kvin aktoj
summaries: "Kiel plaĉas al vi: komedio en kvin aktoj" by William Shakespeare is a pastoral comedy written in 1599. When Rosalind flees her uncle's court seeking safety, she ventures into the Forest of Arden with her cousin Celia. There, they encounter memorable characters including the melancholy Jaques, who delivers Shakespeare's famous "All the world's a stage" speech. In this forest refuge, Rosalind discovers not only sanctuary but also the possibility of love, while Jaques provides sharp observations that contrast with the other characters' perspectives on life.
